principle: DON'T TRUST, VERIFY. A threat class is only 'verified' when (a) a gate exists AND (b) a self-test proves that gate actually BLOCKS the bug (inject a violation -> the gate must go red). A gate with no proof-it-blocks self-test is TRUSTED-NOT-VERIFIED: we believe it works but have never confirmed it. This ledger maps every threat class to its gate + self-test, and ci/check_threat_gate_coverage.py enforces it: a covered threat whose gate file is missing is a BLOCKING error; a covered gate with no self-test is reported as an unverified-trust gap to be closed; a class with no gate at all is a coverage GAP.

status_legend.verified: gate exists AND a self-test proves it blocks the bug class
status_legend.trusted: gate exists but no self-test proves it blocks (don't-trust-verify gap)
status_legend.gap: no gate exists for this threat class

threats.class: Constant-time: secret-dependent branch / cmov-to-branch at source level
Constant-time: GPU warp/lane divergence on secret data
Constant-time: compiler-introduced / microarchitectural timing leak (BINARY level)
threats.note: Welch t-test binary-CT leak detector. DETECTOR is verified: ci/test_dudect_ct_probe.py proves it flags a synthetic +12-cycle leak (|t|~94) and passes uniform timing (|t|~0.6); the live --samples path is validated end-to-end. LIVE measurement against a real CT-timing binary is ADVISORY (returns 77 without samples) due to CI DVFS/SMT noise; audit suite exploit_eucleak_inversion_timing covers ct::scalar_inverse the same way. Source-level companion: ct-source-branch (check_ct_branches.py).

threats.class: Secret-derived stack residue not scrubbed (secure_erase)
threats.note: Blind-zone #7: scope extended from 2 shim dirs to ALSO cover the ufsecp ABI impl layer (src/cpu/src/impl, scalar_parse_strict_nonzero on privkey) where seckeys are actually parsed — 45 fns across 21 files, all erase; 10 critical paths pinned. Self-test proves it flags a seckey parse with no secure_erase in BOTH the shim and ufsecp-impl spellings. Also ci/dev_bug_scanner.py::check_secret_unerased (hardened 2026-06-11).

threats.class: Signing nonce not erased after use (BIP-327)
Private key parsed non-strict (reduces mod n instead of rejecting)
Key/nonce material drawn from a degraded or fail-open entropy source (duplicate/weak CSPRNG)
threats.note: Single-source + fail-closed: csprng_fill may be defined only in detail/csprng.hpp and must return void + abort on RNG failure (no fail-open bool a caller can ignore). Caught bip39.cpp's local fail-open duplicate (blind-zone #5, fixed to use the canonical helper; regression_bip39_csprng_failclosed). Weak-RNG (random_device/mt19937/rand) in the secret core is reported with a triaged allowlist (batch-verify public weights, shim aux_rand hedging). Self-test proves a second def + a fail-open canonical block.

threats.class: Tagged-hash domain tag collision / cross-protocol replay
Cross-backend copy-paste divergence (source level)
Backend porting bug: GPU computed value != CPU on the same input (RUNTIME)
threats.note: Ledger docs/BACKEND_DIFFERENTIAL_OPS.json maps each GPU op to its runtime CPU<->GPU value-differential probe (3 covered: exploit_gpu_cpu_divergence, exploit_backend_divergence, gpu_zk_prove_verify_differential; 2 roadmap: bip352-scan, batch-sign). DETECTOR (byte comparator + coverage check) is VERIFIED: self-test proves it flags a one-byte/length divergence and blocks an unwired covered probe. LIVE CPU<->GPU run is ADVISORY (no GPU on default runners; carried by the covered audit modules on GPU hosts). Source companion: backend-parity-source (check_backend_parity.py).

threats.class: verify==OK on a FORGED/unsound input (the GHSA-c7q2 class)
threats.note: Negative-soundness forge probes per protocol; ledger docs/SOUNDNESS_INVARIANTS.json.

threats.class: Protocol metamorphic relation broken (adapt(sign) valid; extract(adapt)=witness; aggregate=single-sign; Pedersen homomorphism)
threats.note: Positive complement to the soundness (negative) gate. Ledger docs/METAMORPHIC_RELATIONS.json; covered probes: metamorphic_adaptor (adapt/extract correspondence) + exploit_pedersen_homomorphism.

threats.class: Protocol invariant violation (FROST threshold, etc.)
Memory error on fixed tests (UAF, OOB, uninit, leak, race)
Unlock-then-use raw owning-pointer escape from a lock-guarded container (UAF: PRECOMPUTE-GCONTEXT-UAF / shim ka_get class)
threats.note: Static gate: a function holding std::lock_guard/scoped_lock/unique_lock must not `return it->second.get()` (raw handle escaping the critical section); return a shared_ptr snapshot instead. Caught the two confirmed UAFs (precompute g_context, shim_musig ka_get); blind-zone #4 fixed by making g_ka shared_ptr + snapshot accessors (regression_musig_keyagg_lifetime). Self-test proves it flags the bug shape and passes the fix.

threats.class: Memory/logic bug reachable only by coverage-guided fuzzing of parsers/ABI
threats.note: 11 libFuzzer harnesses (5 ClusterFuzzLite src/cpu/fuzz + 6 audit CTest) with seed corpora + .clusterfuzzlite continuous campaign. Wiring gate proves no harness is silently dead / no dangling build ref (fast, in run_fast_gates); check_fuzz_smoke.sh builds+runs a harness with clang -fsanitize=fuzzer,address to prove the layer is alive (heavier, --full). Self-test proves the wiring gate blocks a dead harness.

threats.class: Hostile/confused caller (NULL, overlap, count==0, double-free)
Signing nonce reuse / predictable nonce
Single-fault skip of a verify/branch yields a forged success
threats.note: Ledger docs/FAULT_COUNTERMEASURE_LEDGER.json maps each critical signing path to its sign-then-verify countermeasure + fault probe (4 covered: ecdsa/schnorr_sign_verified + 2 DFA probes; 1 roadmap: musig2/frost partial-sign). Gate enforces the countermeasure still EXISTS and re-verifies (catches a hollowed-out *_sign_verified) and the fault probe is wired. Self-test proves it blocks a hollowed/deleted countermeasure + an unwired probe.

threats.class: Core math invariant unproven (field/group laws)
Exploit PoC not wired into the runner (silently dead)
A src/cpu security fix lands with no test
Dependency / build-provenance tampering
